title: Holding Foreign Insiders Accountable Act
plain_summary: This bill makes new rules for foreign companies that sell their stock in America. These foreign companies must follow the same rules that American companies already follow when they share information about their business. The people who run these foreign companies and the people who own a lot of their stock must report when they buy or sell shares. This is the same thing that American company leaders and big shareholders have to do. The goal is to make sure foreign companies are just as open about their stock trading as American companies are.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Holding Foreign Insiders Accountable Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Disclosures by Directors, Officers, and Principal Stockholders
sections.text: This section changes an existing law called the Securities Exchange Act of nineteen thirty-four. The change adds new words to make it clear that the disclosure rules also apply to foreign private issuers. A foreign private issuer is a company from another country that sells stock in the United States. The new words say that directors, officers, and major stockholders of these foreign companies must follow the same reporting rules as American companies. If any existing government regulation conflicts with this new rule, that conflicting regulation will no longer be valid. Within ninety days after this law is passed, the Securities and Exchange Commission must create final rules or change existing rules to put this new requirement into effect.
